---
title: "Course Orientation & Syllabus Overview"
subtitle: "MHIA 6160 | Value & Quality in Healthcare | W27"
title-slide-attributes:
  data-background-color: "#b3cde0"
author: "Dr. Cameron B. Chiarot"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    footer: "Schulich School of Business, York University | Copyright © 2027 Cameron B. Chiarot | MHIA 6160"
    embed-resources: true
    controls: true        # Displays visible forward/backward navigation arrows
    progress: true        # Displays a visual progress bar along the bottom
    slide-number: true    # Shows the current slide number (e.g., 3/25)
    width: 1600           # Widens the slide canvas (default is 1050)
    height: 900           # Establishes a standard laptop aspect ratio
    auto-stretch: true    # Automatically scales down large elements to fit
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        /* Lock font size over theme presets */
        #title-slide .title { 
          font-size: 60pt !important; 
        }
        #title-slide .subtitle { 
          font-size: 36pt !important; 
        }
        #title-slide .quarto-title-author-name { 
          font-size: 36pt !important; 
        }
        </style>
---

## Welcome to Value & Quality in Healthcare

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="80%"}
### Course Essentials
* **Course Code & Term:** MHIA 6160 | Winter 2027
* **Credits & Duration:** 1.5 Credits | 6 Weeks | 3-Hour Sessions
* **Lecture Schedule:** Wednesdays | 6:30 - 9:30 PM ET
* **Format:** In-person | Synchronous
* **Location:** Schulich School of Business | York University (Keele)
* **Textbook:** None
* **Prerequisites:** Enthusiasm

:::

::: {.column width="20%"}
::: {.callout-tip icon=true}
### Office Hours
* Wednesdays
* 6:00 - 6:30 PM ET
* Lecture Room
:::
:::

:::

---

## Instructor Profile

#### Industry
* **Experience:** 20+ years Healthcare Administration (Public & Private Sectors).
* **Portfolio:** Research, Org. Transformations, P&L, Capital >$100 M, Operations

#### Research
* **Research Fields:** Health Economics, Epidemiology, and Biostatistics.
* **Core Research:** Global Health, Macro- & Microeconomics, Health System Resilience

#### Education
* **PhD** - School of Public Health Sciences, University of Waterloo
* **MBA** - Schulich School of Business, York University

---

## Course Core Premise

::: {.callout-tip icon=true}
### Streamlined Strategy
How do we navigate the complex interface between public health mandates and private-sector innovations?
:::

### Beyond Standard Definitions
* This course delineates the principal components of a **value-based** healthcare system.

* Understanding and managing components and explore their application within the **Canadian healthcare** framework.

* Apply a **dual-lens** strategy to navigate the complex interface between Canada’s public health mandates and private-sector innovations.

* The goal is to synthesize high-level theoretical frameworks with the practical realities of health system leadership.

---

## The Two Pedagogical Pillars

### 1. The Dual-Lens Strategy (Theory & Practice)

* **The System Lens (Public Sector):** Focuses on macro-level equity, the Canada Health Act (CHI), population-centred care, and regulatory frameworks.

* **The Industry Lens (Private Sector):** Focuses on micro-level efficiency, technological innovation, fiscal sustainability, and competitive market drivers.

### 2. The Code-Along Philosophy (Applied Evidence)

* Data literacy for leadership and **not** a computer science class.

* Emphasis on modifying parameters, interpreting outputs, and evidence-based decisions. 

---

## The Capstone

### Simulation-Based Synthesis

* The course concludes with using the **DAEDALUS Explore** modelling interface.

* Act as a designated **Taskforce** managing a real-time global health crisis.

* **The Challenge:** Model critical trade-offs between public health interventions and macroeconomic stability.

* **The Deliverable:** Defend your strategy to an executive-level audience.

---

## Six-Week Curriculum Map

<br>

* **Module 1:** Foundations of Value & Quality (The Value Framework)

* **Module 2:** Measuring Value & Quality (M. Porter)

* **Module 3:** Population Health & Delivery Innovations (Value-Based)

* **Module 4:** Health System Sustainability & Managing Risk

* **Module 5:** Digital Infrastructure & AI Strategy

* **Module 6:** Implementation Leadership & Stakeholder Strategy

---

## Assessment Portfolio Overview

<br>

| Component | Weight % | Due Week | Evaluation Type |
| :--- | :---: | :---: | :--- |
| **Quizzes (x3)** | 15 (5 ea) | 2, 3, 4 | Individual |
| **Case Analysis** | 30 | 3 | Individual |
| **R Visual Brief** | 15 | 4 | Individual |
| **Capstone 1A (Report)** | 20 | 5 | Taskforce |
| **Capstone 1B (Summit)** | 20 | 5 or 6 | Taskforce |

---

## Course Assessments

### Start-of-Class Quizzes (15% Total)

* 15 minutes, 10 multiple-choice questions administered via MySchulich.

* Based strictly on the **mandatory** weekly readings.

* Administered at the start of class; **no make-ups**.

### Case Analysis (30%)

* 1,000–1,500 word comparative critique of two organizations.

* Formally structured around the foundational **Value Framework**:

$$Value = \frac{Outcomes}{Costs}$$

---

## Course Assessments (cont.)

### R-Based Visualizations (15%)

* Objective: Convert raw health data into professional figures.

* Deliverable: **2** R-generated figures and a structured **200-word** analysis per figure.

* Report: Methodology, Rationale, Insights, and Recommendations.

### Capstone Written Brief & Summit Pitch (40%)

* **Component 1A (Written Brief):** A 10-page **executive report** detailing your nationwide response strategy, featuring dual-lens analysis and **2** R-generated simulation plots.

* **Component 1B (Summit Pitch):** A 10-minute presentation followed by a high-stakes 5-minute Q & A defending your strategic choices to the National Cabinet.

---

## Executive Standards

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

### The Executive Briefing

* **Bottom Line Upfront:** Always state key insights and recommendations at the immediate outset of reports.

* **Structural Clarity:** Use descriptive headings to respect the time of busy decision-makers.

* **Neutral, Authoritative Tone:** Base arguments strictly on objective value and quality metrics, never anecdotes.

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

### Course Policies

* **Late Penalty:** Late submissions face a **10% deduction per day** unless formal arrangements are pre-approved for documented medical needs.

::: {.callout-important icon=true}
### Academic Integrity
Individual work must be entirely original.
:::
:::

:::

---

## Next Steps: Getting Ready for the R-Labs

### Software Prerequisites

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="60%"}
1. **Install R:** Download the version matching your `OS`.
2. **Install RStudio Desktop:** Download the free desktop edition from Posit.
3. **Verify Setup:** Open `RStudio` and ensure the `R` version prints in the console.
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
::: {.callout-tip icon=true}
### Local File Hygiene
Build an `R Project` (`.Rproj`) to securely anchor your working directories.
:::
:::

:::

---

## Student Evaluation {background-color="#caf0f8"}

* Following our Week 1 R-Laboratory, we will dedicate the last 10 minutes for student participation in a Week 1 Evaluation. The objectives of the evaluation are:

* **Objective 1:** Students will complete a short Evaluation of the Week 1 curriculum, including the Module 1 lecture material and the R-Laboratory session. Student **responses are anonymous** and are critical for improving the delivery and structure of future lecture modules and R-Laboratories.

* **Objective 2:** Student anonymized, aggregated data from these evaluations is being collected for a formal research study on educational pedagogy and executive data literacy in health systems leadership. Participation in the research portion is **completely voluntary** and will have absolutely **no impact on your course grade**. 


**Note:** *Two additional Student Evaluations will be completed during Weeks 4 and 6. The Evaluations will be completed following the R-Laboratory (Week 4) and the Lecture (Week 6)*

---

## Capstone Taskforce (Group Work) {background-color="#caf0f8"}

* Following our Week 1 Student Evaluation, we will dedicate the last 20 minutes of the class for the Capstone Taskforce formation.

* **Objective:** Form a Taskforce of **5** members.

* **Deliverable:** Email the instructor of your Taskforce members.

<br>

### Email Instructions

**Subject Heading:** MHIA 6160 Capstone Taskforce

**Email:** [Dr. Chiarot](cbchiarot@uwaterloo.ca)

**Note:** Only one Taskforce member is required to email the instructor.

---

## Intentionally Blank {background-color="#b3cde0" .center}

**{end}**
